meta.title: Novogradac LIHTC Equity Pricing Benchmarks
meta.methodology: Novogradac surveys syndicators monthly and publishes national + regional average equity pricing for both 9% (competitive) and 4% (bond-driven) LIHTC. Pricing reflects: target IRR (typically 5-7% for 9%, 4-6% for 4%), CRA-buyer demand premium, asset-class basis-boost mix, sponsor track record, and macro debt-cost (perm rate + cap-rate impact on residual). Pricing is the cents-per-credit-dollar an investor pays for $1 of 10-yr tax credit + depreciation deductions; e.g., $0.87 means $0.87 of equity per $1.00 of 10-yr LIHTC credit annual amount.
meta.important_disclaimer: Pricing benchmarks below are COHO's best-effort directional estimates based on publicly-available Novogradac reporting and recent market commentary. For IC memos, investor presentations, or actual capital-stack sizing, ALWAYS pull the current Novogradac Journal of Tax Credits pricing article directly. Pricing moves 1-4 cents per quarter based on macro conditions (10y Treasury, tax-credit policy uncertainty, CRA-buyer appetite) and 3-5 cents based on deal-specific factors (location, sponsor, BR mix, AMI mix). Use these as a starting anchor, not a closing number.

national_avg.notes: 9% credits trade at a premium to 4% because the per-deal credit amount is larger relative to TDC (more equity per deal), and 9% deals carry stronger demand from CRA-buyers. Spread has compressed from ~$0.05 (2023) to ~$0.02 (2026) as bond-deal supply has grown.

northeast.notes: NYC + Boston metros consistently price 3-5 cents above national average due to CRA-buyer concentration.

southeast.notes: Atlanta + Tampa + Charlotte at national average; rural Southeast 2-3 cents discount.

mountain_west.notes: Denver, Salt Lake, Phoenix metros at this level. Rural Colorado and Wyoming typically 2-4 cents below — fewer CRA-buyers, smaller deal sizes, weaker secondary trading.

california.notes: LA/Bay Area highest national pricing — CRA-buyer concentration + state-credit stacking.

denver_msa.notes: Denver-Aurora-Lakewood MSA tracks Mountain West average.

rural_colorado.notes: Rural CO counties (SLV, Eastern Plains, Northwest, Southwest, South Central) typically price 3-4 cents below MSA. Fewer CRA-buyers, smaller deal sizes, exit-strategy uncertainty. Stack state LIHTC and Prop 123 to compensate.

resort_communities.notes: Aspen, Telluride, Crested Butte etc. — strong workforce-housing investor demand offsets smaller deal sizes.

what_moves_pricing_up: Falling 10y Treasury yield (debt becomes cheaper → cap rates fall → residual value rises → investor IRR up)
CRA reform creating new buyer demand
Tax-credit policy certainty (e.g., extending placed-in-service windows, raising 9% credit cap)
Strong sponsor track record (CHFA-allocator-tier syndicators get premium)
Larger deals (50+ units, $20M+ TDC) get premium of 1-2 cents over smaller
what_moves_pricing_down: Bonus-depreciation phase-down (Section 168(k) drops by 20% per year)
Tax-rate cut concerns (lower top-rate = lower credit value)
Rural / smaller / first-time-sponsor / non-traditional populations discount

validation_check.verified_against: Novogradac.com public-facing equity pricing summary (subscription required for full report)
validation_check.next_review: Quarterly — refresh data + verify against current Novogradac publication
validation_check.ingestion_note: Future: build a script that pulls the public Novogradac monthly headline article + parses national 9%/4% from the lede. Manual quarterly update for now.
